original_query: select s_name, count(*) as numwait from supplier, lineitem l1, orders, nation where s_suppkey = l1.l_suppkey and o_orderkey = l1.l_orderkey and o_orderstatus = 'F' and l1.l_receiptdate > l1.l_commitdate and exists ( select * from lineitem l2 where l2.l_orderkey = l1.l_orderkey and l2.l_suppkey <> l1.l_suppkey ) and not exists ( select * from lineitem l3 where l3.l_orderkey = l1.l_orderkey and l3.l_suppkey <> l1.l_suppkey and l3.l_receiptdate > l3.l_commitdate ) and s_nationkey = n_nationkey and n_name = 'INDONESIA' group by s_name order by numwait desc, s_name limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH order_aggregates AS (
    SELECT 
        l.l_orderkey,
        COUNT(DISTINCT l.l_suppkey) AS total_suppliers,
        COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN l.l_receiptdate > l.l_commitdate THEN l.l_suppkey END) AS delayed_suppliers_count
    FROM lineitem l
    JOIN orders o ON l.l_orderkey = o.o_orderkey AND o.o_orderstatus = 'F'
    GROUP BY l.l_orderkey
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT l.l_suppkey) >= 2 
        AND COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN l.l_receiptdate > l.l_commitdate THEN l.l_suppkey END) = 1
),
delayed_lineitems AS (
    SELECT l.l_orderkey, l.l_suppkey
    FROM lineitem l
    JOIN order_aggregates oa ON l.l_orderkey = oa.l_orderkey
    WHERE l.l_receiptdate > l.l_commitdate
)
SELECT s.s_name, COUNT(*) AS numwait
FROM supplier s
JOIN nation n ON s.s_nationkey = n.n_nationkey AND n.n_name = 'INDONESIA'
JOIN delayed_lineitems dl ON s.s_suppkey = dl.l_suppkey
GROUP BY s.s_name
ORDER BY numwait DESC, s.s_name
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.produced_suggestion: Replace correlated EXISTS and NOT EXISTS subqueries with CTEs that precompute aggregated conditions
Use CTEs to avoid multiple scans of the large lineitem table
Filter orders early by joining with orders table in the CTE and applying the o_orderstatus = 'F' condition
Limit the delayed_lineitems CTE to only scan lineitems for orders that meet the aggregation conditions
